topic: limited supply, strong demand, and wine price pressure
trigger_keywords: precios de los vinos de Borgoña Grand Cru
superficie total de los Grand Cru es muy limitada
demanda global supera ampliamente la oferta

steps.label: cause
steps.text: Production is structurally limited while demand from buyers is substantially greater than the available supply.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: More buyers compete for a small quantity of wine, increasing willingness to pay and strengthening producers' and intermediaries' pricing power.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: Scarcity combined with strong demand places upward pressure on market prices, alongside other influences such as reputation, vintage, and distribution.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: When a fixed, limited supply faces demand that greatly exceeds it, competition for the wine tends to raise its market price.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
